chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 335 of 2020 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 27.05.2021 Mr. Taimur Ali Mirza, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Through the instant petition, petitioner Muhammad Younus has prayed for the grant of Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration in respect of moveable and immoveable properties/assets, details whereof are mentioned in the Schedule of Properties attached with the memo of petition, available at page 143-145, which were left by his deceased sister, namely, Sultana Hanif, who allegedly died on 24th June, 2020 at Karachi, leaving behind her seven legal heirs, details whereof are mentioned in paragraph No. 3 of the memo of petition. Original Death Certificate of deceased as well as photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA, title documents of the immovable properties and Meezan Aamdan Certificates are also filed at page 13 to 17 and 27 to 135. Legal heirs No. 2 to 7 have filed their Affidavits of No-Objection for the grant of Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration in favour of the petitioner/legal heir No.1, which are available on record. Two witnesses, namely, Sohail Ahmed and Nadeem Ahmed have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 19th October, 2020; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of 2 Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 5th November, 2020.

Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 19th October, 2020; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of 2 Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 5th November, 2020. On 24th February, 2021 petitioner and legal heirs No. 2 to 7 as well above named witnesses appeared before this Court and affirmed the contents of memo of petition as well as their respective affidavits and also verified their signatures made thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Succession Certificate and Letter of Administration in respect of movable and immovable properties/assets shown in the Schedule of Properties, as per rules. J U D G E Athar Zai
